items.title: Layers and Neurons
items.description: The perceptron is the atomic compute unit defined in Layers and Neurons — one neuron performing weighted sum, bias, and activation. A network with no hidden layers is essentially a single perceptron (or logistic unit with different activation).

items.title: Vectors and Spaces
items.description: Inputs x and weights w are vectors; the score wᵀx is a dot product in feature space. Vectors and Spaces defines vector addition, scaling, and geometric interpretation — the perceptron applies that math to classify points by which side of a hyperplane they lie on.

items.title: Matrix Multiplication
items.description: One perceptron uses a dot product wᵀx. Stacking many perceptrons in a layer replaces repeated dots with a matrix multiply X @ W + b — the batch view used in MLPs and deep learning frameworks.

items.title: Logistic Regression
items.description: Both learn linear boundaries wᵀx + b, but logistic regression uses sigmoid activation and BCE loss for smooth probabilities; the perceptron uses a step function and mistake-driven updates. Same boundary family, different output and training objective.

items.title: Support Vector Machines
items.description: Both produce linear separators in input space. SVM maximizes geometric margin with hinge slack; the perceptron only seeks any separating hyperplane via incremental error correction. SVM is margin-optimal; perceptron is simpler but not margin-aware.
